{{nihongo|'''Uwato Shrine'''|宇波刀神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

| 画像=[[File:Uwato godo.JPG|250px|宇波刀神社]]
社殿（手前に拝殿、奥に本殿） | 所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|安八郡|Q1204320}}{{jalink|神戸町|Q1203575}}大字神戸八幡956 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=宇波刀大神 | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）論社、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} | 例祭= | 創建=不明 | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=宇波刀神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''宇波刀神社'''（うわとじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|安八郡|Q1204320}}{{jalink|神戸町|Q1203575}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}安八郡宇波刀神社の論社である（別の論社は、安八郡{{jalink|安八町|Q474384}}の{{jalink|宇波刀神社 (安八町)|Q11449527|宇波刀神社}} 「うばとじんじゃ」）。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は郷社。 === 沿革・概略 === *創建時期は不明。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}には於門大明神、岡戸大明神と称されていた。 *{{jalink|明治時代||明治初期}}の{{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}の混乱の際に荒廃し、社殿などもこのころに荒廃し、現在は簡素な本殿と拝殿が残るのみである。 *現在は{{jalink|日吉神社 (神戸町)|Q11505069|日吉神社}}が管理しているという。一説によれば、明治初期の混乱から神体を守るために、宇波刀神社の神体は日吉神社に移されたという。 === 交通機関 === *{{jalink|養老鉄道|Q11666892}}{{jalink|広神戸駅|Q5769897}}より徒歩15分。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うわとしんしや こうと}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:神戸町の歴史]] [[Category:神戸町の建築物]]

社殿（手前に拝殿、奥に本殿） | 所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|安八郡|Q1204320}}{{jalink|神戸町|Q1203575}}大字神戸八幡956 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=宇波刀大神 | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）論社、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} | 例祭= | 創建=不明 | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=宇波刀神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''宇波刀神社'''（うわとじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|安八郡|Q1204320}}{{jalink|神戸町|Q1203575}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。 {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}安八郡宇波刀神社の論社である（別の論社は、安八郡{{jalink|安八町|Q474384}}の{{jalink|宇波刀神社 (安八町)|Q11449527|宇波刀神社}} 「うばとじんじゃ」）。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は郷社。 === 沿革・概略 === *創建時期は不明。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}には於門大明神、岡戸大明神と称されていた。 *{{jalink|明治時代||明治初期}}の{{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}の混乱の際に荒廃し、社殿などもこのころに荒廃し、現在は簡素な本殿と拝殿が残るのみである。 *現在は{{jalink|日吉神社 (神戸町)|Q11505069|日吉神社}}が管理しているという。一説によれば、明治初期の混乱から神体を守るために、宇波刀神社の神体は日吉神社に移されたという。 === 交通機関 === *{{jalink|養老鉄道|Q11666892}}{{jalink|広神戸駅|Q5769897}}より徒歩15分。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うわとしんしや こうと}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:神戸町の歴史]] [[Category:神戸町の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

Name=Uwato Shrine | Image=[[File:Uwato godo.JPG|250px|Uwato Shrine]]
Shrine buildings (the worship hall in front, the main sanctuary at the rear) | Location={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Anpachi District|Q1204320}}{{jalink|Gōdo Town|Q1203575}} Ōaza Gōdo Yahata 956 | Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=Uwato Ōkami | Shrine rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor) candidate shrine, former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} | Annual festival= | Established=Unknown | Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Uwato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Uwato Shrine''' (うわとじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}, {{jalink|Anpachi District|Q1204320}}, {{jalink|Gōdo Town|Q1203575}}. It is a candidate shrine (ronsha) for Uwato Shrine of Anpachi District, Mino Province, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}}. (Another candidate shrine is {{jalink|Uwato Shrine (Anpachi Town)|Q11449527|Uwato Shrine}} “Ubatō-jinja” in {{jalink|Anpachi Town|Q474384}}, Anpachi District.) Under the {{jalink|modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|former shrine ranking system}}, it was classified as a gōsha. === History and overview === *The date of establishment is unknown. During the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was known as Omon Daimyōjin and Okado Daimyōjin. *Amid the turmoil of the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}} in the early {{jalink|Meiji period}}, the shrine fell into ruin; the shrine buildings also deteriorated around this time, and today only a simple main sanctuary and worship hall remain. *It is said that the shrine is currently managed by {{jalink|Hiyoshi Shrine (Gōdo Town)|Q11505069|Hiyoshi Shrine}}. According to one theory, in order to protect the sacred object during the chaos of the early Meiji period, the sacred object of Uwato Shrine was transferred to Hiyoshi Shrine. === Transportation === *About a 15-minute walk from {{jalink|Yōrō Railway|Q11666892}} {{jalink|Hiro-Gōdo Station|Q5769897}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Uwato Shrine Gōdo}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:神戸町の歴史]] [[Category:神戸町の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

Shrine buildings (the worship hall in front, the main sanctuary at the rear) | Location={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Anpachi District|Q1204320}}{{jalink|Gōdo Town|Q1203575}} Ōaza Gōdo Yahata 956 | Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=Uwato Ōkami | Shrine rank={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor) candidate shrine, former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} | Annual festival= | Established=Unknown | Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Uwato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Uwato Shrine''' (うわとじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}, {{jalink|Anpachi District|Q1204320}}, {{jalink|Gōdo Town|Q1203575}}. It is a candidate shrine (ronsha) for Uwato Shrine of Anpachi District, Mino Province, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}}. (Another candidate shrine is {{jalink|Uwato Shrine (Anpachi Town)|Q11449527|Uwato Shrine}} “Ubatō-jinja” in {{jalink|Anpachi Town|Q474384}}, Anpachi District.) Under the {{jalink|modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|former shrine ranking system}}, it was classified as a gōsha. === History and overview === *The date of establishment is unknown. During the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was known as Omon Daimyōjin and Okado Daimyōjin. *Amid the turmoil of the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}} in the early {{jalink|Meiji period}}, the shrine fell into ruin; the shrine buildings also deteriorated around this time, and today only a simple main sanctuary and worship hall remain. *It is said that the shrine is currently managed by {{jalink|Hiyoshi Shrine (Gōdo Town)|Q11505069|Hiyoshi Shrine}}. According to one theory, in order to protect the sacred object during the chaos of the early Meiji period, the sacred object of Uwato Shrine was transferred to Hiyoshi Shrine. === Transportation === *About a 15-minute walk from {{jalink|Yōrō Railway|Q11666892}} {{jalink|Hiro-Gōdo Station|Q5769897}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Uwato Shrine Gōdo}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:神戸町の歴史]] [[Category:神戸町の建築物]]

According to one theory, in order to protect the sacred object during the chaos of the early Meiji period, the sacred object of Uwato Shrine was transferred to Hiyoshi Shrine. === Transportation === *About a 15-minute walk from {{jalink|Yōrō Railway|Q11666892}} {{jalink|Hiro-Gōdo Station|Q5769897}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Uwato Shrine Gōdo}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwato Shrine (Gōdo-chō)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwato Shrine (Gōdo-chō)]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:神戸町の歴史]] [[Category:神戸町の建築物]]

Shrine buildings (the worship hall in front, the main sanctuary at the rear) | Location={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Gōdo Town|Q1203575|WD=Q1203575|1=Gōdo, Gifu}} Ōaza Gōdo Yahata 956 | Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=Uwato Ōkami | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Annual festival= | Established=Unknown | Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Uwato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Uwato Shrine''' (うわとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gōdo Town|Q1203575|WD=Q1203575|1=Gōdo, Gifu}}. It is a candidate shrine (ronsha) for Uwato Shrine of Anpachi District, Mino Province, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. (Another candidate shrine is {{ill|lt=Uwato Shrine (Anpachi Town)|Q11449527|WD=Q11449527|lt=Uwato Shrine|1=Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)}} “Ubatō-jinja” in {{ill|lt=Anpachi Town|Q474384|WD=Q474384|1=Anpachi, Gifu}}, Anpachi District.) Under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a gōsha. === History and overview === *The date of establishment is unknown. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was known as Omon Daimyōjin and Okado Daimyōjin. *Amid the turmoil of the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}} in the early {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}, the shrine fell into ruin; the shrine buildings also deteriorated around this time, and today only a simple main sanctuary and worship hall remain. *It is said that the shrine is currently managed by {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Shrine (Gōdo Town)|Q11505069|WD=Q11505069|lt=Hiyoshi Shrine|1=Hiyoshi Shrine (Gōdo)}}. According to one theory, in order to protect the sacred object during the chaos of the early Meiji period, the sacred object of Uwato Shrine was transferred to Hiyoshi Shrine. === Transportation === *About a 15-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} {{ill|lt=Hiro-Gōdo Station|Q5769897|WD=Q5769897|1=Hiro-Gōdo Station}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwato Shrine (Gōdo-chō)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwato Shrine (Gōdo-chō)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:神戸町の歴史]] [[Category:神戸町の建築物]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Gō-sha|lt=Gō-sha|ja|郷社|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7200005004861 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Uhatono Shrine|lt=Uhatono Shrine|WD=Q135039897|1=Uhatono Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡神戸町大字神戸八幡956', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '岐阜県安八郡神戸町神戸', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:宇波刀神社 (神戸町)]] {{wikidata link|Q17217996}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name=Uwato Shrine | Image=[[File:Uwato godo.JPG|250px|Uwato Shrine]]
Shrine buildings (the worship hall in front, the main sanctuary at the rear) | Location={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Gōdo Town|Q1203575|WD=Q1203575|1=Gōdo, Gifu}} Ōaza Gōdo Yahata 956 | Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=Uwato Ōkami | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Annual festival= | Established=Unknown | Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Uwato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Uwato Shrine''' (うわとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gōdo Town|Q1203575|WD=Q1203575|1=Gōdo, Gifu}}. It is a candidate shrine (ronsha) for Uwato Shrine of Anpachi District, Mino Province, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. (Another candidate shrine is {{ill|lt=Uwato Shrine (Anpachi Town)|Q11449527|WD=Q11449527|lt=Uwato Shrine|1=Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)}} “Ubatō-jinja” in {{ill|lt=Anpachi Town|Q474384|WD=Q474384|1=Anpachi, Gifu}}, Anpachi District.) Under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a gōsha. === History and overview === *The date of establishment is unknown. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was known as Omon Daimyōjin and Okado Daimyōjin. *Amid the turmoil of the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}} in the early {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}, the shrine fell into ruin; the shrine buildings also deteriorated around this time, and today only a simple main sanctuary and worship hall remain. *It is said that the shrine is currently managed by {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Shrine (Gōdo Town)|Q11505069|WD=Q11505069|lt=Hiyoshi Shrine|1=Hiyoshi Shrine (Gōdo)}}. According to one theory, in order to protect the sacred object during the chaos of the early Meiji period, the sacred object of Uwato Shrine was transferred to Hiyoshi Shrine. === Transportation === *About a 15-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} {{ill|lt=Hiro-Gōdo Station|Q5769897|WD=Q5769897|1=Hiro-Gōdo Station}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwato Shrine (Gōdo-chō)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwato Shrine (Gōdo-chō)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kōbe]] [[Category:Architecture of Kōbe Town]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

(Another candidate shrine is {{ill|lt=Uwato Shrine (Anpachi Town)|Q11449527|WD=Q11449527|lt=Uwato Shrine|1=Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)}} “Ubatō-jinja” in {{ill|lt=Anpachi Town|Q474384|WD=Q474384|1=Anpachi, Gifu}}, Anpachi District.) Under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a gōsha. === History and overview === *The date of establishment is unknown. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was known as Omon Daimyōjin and Okado Daimyōjin. *Amid the turmoil of the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}} in the early {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}, the shrine fell into ruin; the shrine buildings also deteriorated around this time, and today only a simple main sanctuary and worship hall remain. *It is said that the shrine is currently managed by {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Shrine (Gōdo Town)|Q11505069|WD=Q11505069|lt=Hiyoshi Shrine|1=Hiyoshi Shrine (Gōdo)}}. According to one theory, in order to protect the sacred object during the chaos of the early Meiji period, the sacred object of Uwato Shrine was transferred to Hiyoshi Shrine. === Transportation === *About a 15-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} {{ill|lt=Hiro-Gōdo Station|Q5769897|WD=Q5769897|1=Hiro-Gōdo Station}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwato Shrine (Gōdo-chō)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwato Shrine (Gōdo-chō)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kōbe]] [[Category:Architecture of Kōbe Town]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

(神戸町)]] {{wikidata link|Q17217996}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name=Uwato Shrine | Image=[[File:Uwato godo.JPG|250px|Uwato Shrine]]
Shrine buildings (the worship hall in front, the main sanctuary at the rear) | Location={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Gōdo Town|Q1203575|WD=Q1203575|1=Gōdo, Gifu}} Ōaza Gōdo Yahata 956 | Coordinates= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity=Uwato Ōkami | Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor) candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Annual festival= | Established=Unknown | Map= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Uwato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Uwato Shrine''' (うわとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, {{ill|lt=Anpachi District|Q1204320|WD=Q1204320|1=Anpachi District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gōdo Town|Q1203575|WD=Q1203575|1=Gōdo, Gifu}}. It is a candidate shrine (ronsha) for Uwato Shrine of Anpachi District, Mino Province, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. (Another candidate shrine is {{ill|lt=Uwato Shrine (Anpachi Town)|Q11449527|WD=Q11449527|lt=Uwato Shrine|1=Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)}} “Ubatō-jinja” in {{ill|lt=Anpachi Town|Q474384|WD=Q474384|1=Anpachi, Gifu}}, Anpachi District.) Under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a gōsha. === History and overview === *The date of establishment is unknown. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was known as Omon Daimyōjin and Okado Daimyōjin. *Amid the turmoil of the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}} in the early {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}, the shrine fell into ruin; the shrine buildings also deteriorated around this time, and today only a simple main sanctuary and worship hall remain. *It is said that the shrine is currently managed by {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Shrine (Gōdo Town)|Q11505069|WD=Q11505069|lt=Hiyoshi Shrine|1=Hiyoshi Shrine (Gōdo)}}. According to one theory, in order to protect the sacred object during the chaos of the early Meiji period, the sacred object of Uwato Shrine was transferred to Hiyoshi Shrine. === Transportation === *About a 15-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} {{ill|lt=Hiro-Gōdo Station|Q5769897|WD=Q5769897|1=Hiro-Gōdo Station}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwato Shrine (Gōdo-chō)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwato Shrine (Gōdo-chō)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kōbe]] [[Category:Architecture of Kōbe Town]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

According to one theory, in order to protect the sacred object during the chaos of the early Meiji period, the sacred object of Uwato Shrine was transferred to Hiyoshi Shrine. === Transportation === *About a 15-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} {{ill|lt=Hiro-Gōdo Station|Q5769897|WD=Q5769897|1=Hiro-Gōdo Station}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwato Shrine (Gōdo-chō)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwato Shrine (Gōdo-chō)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kōbe]] [[Category:Architecture of Kōbe Town]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

It is a candidate shrine (ronsha) for Uwato Shrine of Anpachi District, Mino Province, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. (Another candidate shrine is {{ill|lt=Uwato Shrine (Anpachi Town)|Q11449527|WD=Q11449527|lt=Uwato Shrine|1=Ubanato Shrine (Anpachi Town)}} “Ubatō-jinja” in {{ill|lt=Anpachi Town|Q474384|WD=Q474384|1=Anpachi, Gifu}}, Anpachi District.) Under the {{ill|lt=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a gōsha. === History and overview === *The date of establishment is unknown. During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was known as Omon Daimyōjin and Okado Daimyōjin. *Amid the turmoil of the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}} in the early {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}, the shrine fell into ruin; the shrine buildings also deteriorated around this time, and today only a simple main sanctuary and worship hall remain. *It is said that the shrine is currently managed by {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Shrine (Gōdo Town)|Q11505069|WD=Q11505069|lt=Hiyoshi Shrine|1=Hiyoshi Shrine (Gōdo)}}. According to one theory, in order to protect the sacred object during the chaos of the early Meiji period, the sacred object of Uwato Shrine was transferred to Hiyoshi Shrine. === Transportation === *About a 15-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} {{ill|lt=Hiro-Gōdo Station|Q5769897|WD=Q5769897|1=Hiro-Gōdo Station}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uwato Shrine (Gōdo-chō)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uwato Shrine (Gōdo-chō)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kōbe]] [[Category:Architecture of Kōbe Town]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]
